_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Create learner development, training, and remediation plans
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. Knowledge OP-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-S-004 (DA, cosine: 0.530), AI-T-005 (AI, cosine: 0.490).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (4.9/10). The cross-encoder finds meaningful but bounded overlap — consistent with cross-framework mapping between cybersecurity and data/AI domains where shared governance concepts use different terminology.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: Moderate token-level alignment (F1=0.881). Balanced P/R (0.891/0.872) indicating symmetric token-level overlap.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.6%). The model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other — typical for cross-domain framework mapping where concepts overlap without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder (0.546) exceeds STS (0.488). The independent embeddings find more similarity than cross-attention, suggesting surface-level vocabulary overlap exceeds the deeper semantic relationship.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Near-zero lexical overlap (4.5%). The concepts use almost entirely different vocabulary, confirming the relationship is detected through semantic understanding, not word matching.
